local_checks.raw_manifest_scan: Found 97 local 20260708 SGLang scheduled crawler page snapshots under raw/crawler/sglang-github-closed-issues-prs: 14 issue pages and 83 pull request pages, including 55 page-visible merged PRs and 28 closed-unmerged PRs. Selected only seven source-visible operations pages: #30242/#30450, #24159, #30254, #29834, #29716, and #30255. No external fetch was run.
local_checks.wiki_search: Searched SGLang GitHub Closed Issues And PRs, Inference Runtime Infrastructure, Evaluation Observability Reliability Infrastructure, Network Storage Cluster Infrastructure, AI Infra Coverage Map, coverage-map.json, loop-state.json, ingest.md, and prior parent-2/parent-15 manifests for 30242, 30450, 24159, 30254, 29834, 29716, 30255, DeepSeek-V4 NVFP4, FlashInfer A2A, PD router, Mooncake RDMA, HiCacheFile, metadata cache, and Triton recompilation. The selected July 8 IDs were not already promoted as durable rows before this task.
local_checks.domain_index_scan: ai_infra wiki/index.md already routes this work to existing SGLang, inference-runtime, eval-observability, network-storage, and coverage-map reference pages. No new wiki page or structured accelerator catalog row was needed.
local_checks.prior_manifest_scan: Parent-2 already curated July 6-7 SGLang signals and parent-15 already curated JaguarMicro Yunxiao DPU. Parent-16 is a distinct July 8 SGLang page-level boundary and does not repeat parent-2 issue/PR IDs or the hardware parent sequence.

gap_reason: The local corpus had an uncurated July 8 SGLang scheduled-crawler slice with concrete inference-runtime, PD disaggregation, cache/storage, and observability signals after the API corpus cutoff. The selected pages expose durable source-visible behavior: FlashInfer A2A decode CUDA-graph bucket divergence and DP-synchronized fix context, prefill-error router hang and prefill-unreachable decode abort cleanup boundaries, Mooncake RDMA KV-transfer ordering risk, HiCacheFile metadata-cache behavior, and DSV4 sparse prefill Triton recompilation context. The safe boundary is page-level supplement evidence only.

duplicate_checks.finding: Existing rows covered the API corpus through 2026-06-24, July 1-4 supplement leads, parent-2 July 6-7 leads, and #24456. No existing row covered #30242/#30450, #24159, #30254, #29834, #29716, or #30255 before this task.

Existing runtime coverage already included router abort observability, PD/Mooncake issue leads, NIXL/UCX, HiCache bootstrap-abort cleanup, EAGLE DP attention, routed-output hot-path stalls, and Blackwell confidential inference. Parent-16 adds only the July 8 FlashInfer A2A, PD router, decode abort, HiCacheFile, and DSV4 recompilation signals.

Existing observability coverage included SGLang #23937 metric semantics and #28975 profiling. Parent-16 adds #29716 and #30255 as benchmark-context evidence only, not local benchmark baselines or SLOs.

Existing network/storage coverage covered EFA, FSx, WEKA, Ceph, SPDK, Spectrum-X/RoCE, DPU product signals, and MLCommons method evidence. Parent-16 adds only SGLang Mooncake RDMA KV ordering and HiCacheFile filesystem metadata-cache boundaries.

Inference-runtime, eval-observability, and network-storage layers now cite the parent-16 gap proof and selected July 8 raw pages while preserving KServe, production postmortem, full API refresh, SLO, local benchmark, and exact measured-result gaps.
Added one known source id for the July 8 SGLang page-level operations evidence without mutating unrelated blocked_items or hardware catalog state.

selected_source_backed_signals.surface: DeepSeek-V4 NVFP4 FlashInfer A2A disaggregated decode
selected_source_backed_signals.promoted_claim: Issue #30242 records a GB300 DeepSeek-V4-Pro NVFP4 disaggregated decode illegal-memory-access with TP/DP/EP=16 and FlashInfer A2A; merged PR #30450 attributes it to DP/EP ranks replaying different decode CUDA-graph buckets and fixes the path by using DP-synchronized bucket selection based on the cross-rank max.

The closed-unmerged page records that a prefill non-2xx response can leave decode waiting for KV cache while the router waits for both futures, and proposes racing prefill/decode futures so prefill failure cancels decode early.

The closed-unmerged page records a data-race boundary where an RDMA NIC can read GPU KV memory before CUDA compute-stream writes finish, and proposes enqueue-time CUDA events with transfer-worker synchronization before RDMA writes.

The merged page records a scheduler crash when abort cleanup clears a DecodeRequest kv_receiver while a pending_reqs reference remains, and fixes the path by removing failed requests from pending_reqs and guarding abort against a None receiver.

The merged page records optional client-side positive metadata caching for HiCacheFile storage with TTL, startup scan, write backfill, cache-hit bypass, and eviction invalidation, plus a Lustre benchmark context bounded to the source setup.

The merged page records that C128 metadata capacity and sparse combiner top_k moved from Triton compile-time constants to runtime scalars to avoid context-length-specific synchronous recompilation stalls, with source benchmark and accuracy context.

rejected_or_deferred_signals.reason: Left as raw backlog or duplicate-boundary evidence because they were CI/doc/label churn, shallow implementation pages, unmerged experiments, or model-feature work without a clearer infrastructure operations boundary than the selected subset.
Recorded only as page-level operational evidence and proposed mitigation boundaries; not represented as merged fixes.
Kept as source benchmark context only; not promoted as local benchmark results, SLOs, MLCommons submissions, product rankings, or production guarantees.
Not performed in this parent task; the July 8 scheduled crawler supplement remains page-level evidence without joined comments or PR review comments.

non_promoted_boundaries: No full GitHub API refresh, joined comment corpus, PR review-comment corpus, or release-note corpus was created from the July 8 page snapshots.
No production postmortem, service SLO, alert threshold, local benchmark baseline, MLCommons submission, product ranking, or generalized performance guarantee was promoted.
No compute accelerator structured catalog rows or dependency/code files were modified for this SGLang wiki-only task.
No new external source was fetched, and no crawler subscriptions or Domain Channels were changed.
